I think I need to check if the shell has nohup available. Also, I can background the process and poll it using the kill command with -0. It looks like I need to ensure that ps isn't necessary for this, but using kill -0 should work in this case. Let’s see how this all connects together!
The second timeout had the same root cause: I again used a heavy foreground shell job. The tool call itself was valid, but it violated the runtime pattern for long jobs. I should have launched the sweep asynchronously and polled, or reduced scope further before running synchronously.

I’m correcting that now by running a single lean full-data training job in the background, polling its log, and then verifying the produced /app/model.bin.

import os, time, fasttext
train="/tmp/yelp_train_ft.txt"
test="/tmp/yelp_test_ft.txt"
params=dict(
    input=train,
    lr=0.8,
    epoch=8,
    wordNgrams=2,
    dim=64,
    minn=2,
    maxn=5,
    bucket=200000,
    loss="softmax",
    thread=max(1, os.cpu_count() or 1),
)
print("TRAIN_PARAMS", params, flush=True)
start=time.time()
model=fasttext.train_supervised(**params)
print("TRAIN_SECS", round(time.time()-start,2), flush=True)
res=model.test(test)
print("TEST_RESULT", res, flush=True)
out="/app/model.bin"
if os.path.exists(out):
    os.remove(out)
model.save_model(out)
print("MODEL_SIZE_BYTES", os.path.getsize(out), flush=True)
